mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to simplify the deployment and scaling of machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings. It offers intelligent scaling, optimized routing, and model caching, making it suitable for production-grade AI applications. Key trade-offs include the requirement for specific Kubernetes versions and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

```

> Installs the Model CRD.
> Installs the Inference CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage AI inference workloads.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Inference Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Inference resource to demonstrate how to use KubeAI for serving a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: v1
EOF

```

> Creates an example inference resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: v1
EOF

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeAI operator and the example inference resource to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

kubectl get inference

```

> Lists all pods in the kubeai namespace to verify the operator is running.
> Checks the status of the created inference resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all inference resources and associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> Deletes the kubeai namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your resources. This step upgrades KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get inference -o yaml > backup-inference.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backs up the current inference resources.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Inference" in version "kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Inference CRD may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Inference CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai and adjust the configuration or resources as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI service may not be reachable due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with: kubectl get svc -n kubeai and ensure the network policies allow access.

troubleshooting.title: Inference resource not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Inference resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Inference resource with: kubectl get inference and ensure the spec is correct.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running and the example inference resource in a 'Ready' state when everything is working. Use 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai' and 'kubectl get inference' to verify.

apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
spec:
  model:
    name: example-model
    version: v1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and adequate resources.
